name: omarchy_universal_clipboard
content:
Unified cipboard 
Usuay on Linux, you need Ctrl + Shift + C/V to copy'n'paste in the termina and Ctrl
+ C/V to do it everywhere ese. That's hard to get used to for anyone who hasn't been
born and bred on Linux! So too is the switch from super to ctr, if you're coming from the
Mac.
Omarchy tackes both probems with unified cipboard hotkeys that work (amost)
everywhere. They are:
Hotkey Command
Super + C Copy
Super + X Cut
Super + V Paste
Super + Ctr + V Cipboard history
The one exception to this uniformity is the fie manager Nautius). There you'
unfortunatey have to make do with Ctrl + C/X/V  for cipboard operations.
Cipboard history 
The cipboard history is provided by Waker and works for both text and images. You
trigger it by Super + Ctrl + V, seect your entry with return, and then that' be paced on
the cipboard ready to paste on Super + V .
Universal clipboard · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/105/universal-clipboard

You can aso search the history just by starting to type:
